mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:latest RSAFS value is 387789.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by nonpositive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations or if its 3-year rolling Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 9.0%, labor-market stress signals weak household income and elevated default risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:latest UNRATE value is 9.2 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with UNRATE above 9.0% have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or do not underperform unconditional returns by at least 25 bps.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25% and CPI is not falling over 3 months, accommodative policy lowers discount rates without confirming deflation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
series:CPIAUCSL
fact:latest FEDFUNDS value is 0.09 as of t-1mo
fact:latest CPIAUCSL value is 224.304 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition produces negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered observations or if FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL interaction coefficients are not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in industrial production signals improving factory utilization and operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:latest INDPRO value is 93.07 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO growth is not associated with higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative 3-month INDPRO growth, or if the return spread is negative in two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 600 thousand units, weak residential construction signals depressed credit creation and durable-goods demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:latest HOUST value is 560.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below 600 is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or if triggered returns exceed unconditional returns by more than 50 bps.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI acceleration occurs while the federal funds rate remains below 0.25%, inflation pressure erodes real margins and raises future tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 6-month annualized CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 12-month CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS < 0.25%
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest CPIAUCSL value is 224.304 as of t-1mo
fact:latest FEDFUNDS value is 0.09 as of t-1mo
regime_hint: zero-rate regime with rising inflation pressure
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI acceleration and low-rate interaction is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or if interaction alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment rises over 3 months while unemployment remains above 8.0%, early labor repair signals cyclical earnings recovery from a depressed base, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
series:UNRATE
fact:latest PAYEMS value is 131017.0 as of t-1mo
fact:latest UNRATE value is 9.2 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not outperform unconditional 60-day S&P 500 returns by at least 50 bps or if positive triggered returns occur in fewer than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises by more than 25 basis points over 3 months, tighter short-rate policy raises discount rates and reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest FEDFUNDS value is 0.09 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS tightening triggers are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if triggered returns are not lower than non-triggered returns.
